overview: A single Go-based HTTP service exposing a minimal Hello World REST API. The service handles root, hello, and health endpoints with proper HTTP method and status code handling, deployed as a Docker container on Kubernetes.
requirements: Expose GET /hello returning 'Hello, World!' in plain text with status 200
Expose GET / returning a welcome message
Respond to content with content-type text/plain

components.name: hello-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Minimal Hello World HTTP API
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Welcome message
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Welcome message
          content:
            text/plain:
              schema:
                type: string
  /hello:
    get:
      summary: Returns Hello, World!
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Hello message
          content:
            text/plain:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: Hello, World!
        '405':
          description: Method not allowed
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            text/plain:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a Go HTTP service using net/http listening on port 9090.

Endpoints:
- GET / → 200, text/plain, body: 'Welcome to the Hello World API'
- GET /hello → 200, Content-Type: text/plain, body: 'Hello, World!'
- Non-GET on /hello → 405 Method Not Allowed (set Allow: GET header)
- GET /health → 200, text/plain, body: 'OK' (must respond in <100ms; no external calls)
- Any other path → 404 Not Found

Implementation details:
- Use http.ServeMux with explicit handlers; in the /hello handler check r.Method and return 405 if not GET.
- In the root handler, check r.URL.Path == '/' exactly, else return 404.
- Set Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8 on all responses.
- Use http.Server with ReadTimeout=5s, WriteTimeout=10s, IdleTimeout=60s.
- Log each request (method, path, status) to stdout.
- Include graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.

Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.22-alpine for build and alpine or distroless for runtime. EXPOSE 9090. Binary runs as non-root.

Module name: hello-api. Single main.go is acceptable. Ensure the service supports at least 100 concurrent requests (default net/http goroutine-per-request is sufficient).
